home.title: Portfolio - Candice Carton
home.heading: Candice Carton
home.subtitle: Computer Science Student
intro: As a second-year Computer Science student at the IUT of Clermont-Ferrand, I specialize in mobile and web development, with complementary skills in database management and interactive experience design.

I am looking for a work-study position for my third year, with a focus on mobile development. Passionate about Japanese culture, anime, and game design, these interests shape my creative perspective and enrich the way I approach interface and user experience design.

sections.title: Reading
paragraphs: I love reading, whether it is novels, manga, or manhwa. Reading lets me escape, discover new worlds, and enrich my imagination.

sections.title: Video games & esports
paragraphs: Video games play an important role in my life. I especially enjoy open-world solo games. I am also very interested in esports for its team spirit, strategy, and the passion shared by players and communities.

sections.title: Anime & Films
paragraphs: I am passionate about anime, films, and series. I enjoy discovering Japanese works as well as films from around the world for their creativity and the emotions they convey. Fantasy and science fiction are my favorite genres, but I also enjoy dramas and comedies.

Some anime left a mark on me because their settings were inspired by real places in Japan. I love recognizing these places in the stories, and I have already been able to visit some of them, such as Your Name and Suzume.

sections.title: Japanese culture
paragraphs: I truly enjoy Japanese cuisine: sushi, ramen, okonomiyaki, mochi, and more. I like discovering new flavors and trying to cook traditional dishes at home. The presentation of the dishes and the balance of flavors are aspects I especially appreciate.

Japan is also fascinating for its temples, gardens, festivals, and customs. I admire the beauty of Shinto shrines, the serenity of zen gardens, and the richness of traditional celebrations such as Hanami and Tanabata. I had the chance to travel alone in Japan during a language stay with EF Education, which allowed me to discover these cultural aspects firsthand and deepen my interest in the country.

projects.title: Projects - Candice Carton
projects.heading: My Projects
projects.intro: A selection of my academic and personal work, spanning mobile development, databases, game design, and immersive experiences.

results.zero: No projects match the selected filters.

cards.title: Livradois-Forez Forest Mapping
paragraphs:
This project aims to provide a tree mapping solution for the Livradois-Forez association. The application allows users to consult information about species and diseases, and to add, edit, or delete entries based on field monitoring needs.
The team delivered several connected components: a Kotlin mobile app, a PHP web app, a Blazor admin interface, and an API linking everything together.
I mainly worked on account authentication on the PHP side and on tree data management with EF. My contribution was cut short when I left for my semester in Canada.
Technologies used: Blazor, PHP, Entity Framework, ASP.NET, Kotlin.

cards.title: Rep'It - Mobile Fitness Routine App
paragraphs:
This Android app, built in Kotlin by a two-person team, helps users organize workout routines and stay consistent over time.
The goal is to provide simple tracking for sessions, exercises, and weekly habits through a clear interface designed for everyday use.
In the longer term, the app is intended to connect to a weather API, include a statistics system, and personalize daily challenges according to user preferences and current conditions.
Technologies used: Kotlin, Android Studio, XML, weather API (planned).
captions: Overview of active and completed challenges

cards.title: Black Out - Immersive VR Experience
paragraphs:
Black Out is a VR project developed by a team of five in Unity3D and designed as an immersive virtual reality experience.
The player is placed inside a house with no electricity and must restore the power by finding the key to the room containing the electrical panel.
The game relies on pareidolia and audio cues to heighten tension, distort perception, and deepen immersion throughout the experience.
Technologies used: Unity3D.

cards.title: VR Weather Station Connected to an API
paragraphs:
Created in Unity as a functional prototype, this project focused on retrieving weather data from an API and displaying it inside a VR environment.
The visual aspect was intentionally secondary. The main objective was to make the weather information clear and visible in the scene, especially on a television in a virtual living room.
The final result demonstrates end-to-end integration between Unity and the API, with dynamic weather updates displayed in 3D.
Technologies used: Unity3D, weather API (HTTP/JSON requests).

cards.title: Video Game Design Project
paragraphs:
This solo game design project revolves around Yume no Fukushuu, imagined as an action-adventure experience for PC and consoles.
The goal was to design a complete concept and present it in a clear one-pager. The project also includes an original lore that goes further than what is shown in the summary document.
The concept features five main bosses and emphasizes strong consistency between narrative, worldbuilding, and gameplay intent.
Deliverable: one-pager.
